translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having stayed at Mathurā that night[1] the highly intelligent Akrūra mounted a chariot and proceeded to Nanda’s Vraja.

devanagari: गच्छन् पथि महाभागो भगवत्यम्बुजेक्षणे । भक्तिं परामुपगत एवमेतदचिन्तयत् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: gacchan pathi mahābhāgo bhagavatyambujekṣaṇe bhaktiṃ parāmupagata evametadacintayat
translation: On his way, the highly fortunate Akrūra who was filled with extreme devotion to the lotus-eyed Lord reflected to himself as follows:—

devanagari: किं मयाऽचरितं भद्रं किं तप्तं परमं तपः । किं वाथाप्यर्हते दत्तं यद् द्रक्ष्याम्यद्य केशवम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ mayā kiṃ vāthāpyarhate dattaṃ yad drakṣyāmyadya keśavam

translation: “What auspicious act has been done by me! What austere penance I have performed! Or what gift to a deserving person has been donated by me, by virtue of which I am going to see Lord Kṛṣṇa today.

devanagari: ममैतद् दुर्लभं मन्ये उत्तमःश्लोकदर्शनम् । विषयात्मनो यथा ब्रह्म कीर्तनं शूद्रजन्मनः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: mamaitad durlabhaṃ manye uttamaḥślokadarśanam viṣayātmano yathā brahma kīrtanaṃ śūdrajanmanaḥ

translation: Just as recitation and study of the Vedas is impossible to a person born in the Śūḍra class, I consider that gaining even the sight of Kṛṣṇa of excellent renown is very difficult for me, inasmuch as my mind is addicted to sensual pleasures.

devanagari: मैवं ममाधमस्यापि स्यादेवाच्युतदर्शनम् । ह्रियमाणः कलनद्या क्वचित्तरति कश्चन ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: maivaṃ mamādhamasyāpi syādevācyutadarśanam hriyamāṇaḥ kalanadyā kvacittarati kaścana
translation: Perhaps that may not happen that way. Even though I may be a vile person, I may be blessed with the sight of the Immortal Lord Kṛṣṇa. Sometimes a person carried along with the current of time, may get to the other shore of saṃsāra.

devanagari: ममाद्यामङ्गलं नष्टं फलवांश्चैव मे भवः । यन्नमस्ये भगवतो योगिध्येयांघ्रिपङ्कजम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: mamādyāmaṅgalaṃ naṣṭaṃ phalavāṃścaiva me bhavaḥ yannamasye bhagavato yogidhyeyāṃghripaṅkajam

translation: To-day all my evils (sins) are destroyed, and my birth has achieved its highest objective in that now I shall bow down to the glorious Lord’s lotus-feet which are contemplated

devanagari: कंसो बताद्याकृत मेऽत्यनुग्रहं । द्रक्ष्येऽङ्घ्रिपद्मं प्रहितोऽमुना हरेः । कृतावतारस्य दुरत्ययं तमः । पूर्वेऽतरन् यन्नखमण्डलत्विषा ॥ ७ ॥

translation: Verily Kaṃsa has shown a great favour to me as, being deputed by him, I shall see the lotus-feet of Lord Hari who has now incarnated in this world and by the lustre of whose circle of toe-nails, people in the former ages have got over the darkness of Saṃsāra which is so very difficult to cross.

devanagari: यदर्चितं ब्रह्मभवादिभिः सुरैः । श्रिया च देव्या मुनिभिः ससात्वतैः । गोचारणायानुचरैश्चरद्वने । यद्‍गोपिकानां कुचकुङ्कुमाङ्कितम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: yadarcitaṃ brahmabhavādibhiḥ suraiḥ śriyā ca devyā munibhiḥ sasātvataiḥ gocāraṇāyānucaraiścaradvane yad‍gopikānāṃ kucakuṅkumāṅkitam

translation: The Lord’s feet are worshipped by gods Brahmā, Śiva and others, by goddess Lakṣmī, by sages and by Sātvatas (Yādavas or his devotees)—feet which wandered in the forest along with his companions for grazing the cows and which are tinged with the saffron applied to the breasts of Gopīs.

devanagari: द्रक्ष्यामि नूनं सुकपोलनासिकं । स्मितावलोकारुणकञ्जलोचनम् । मुखं मुकुन्दस्य गुडालकावृतं । प्रदक्षिणं मे प्रचरन्ति वै मृगाः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: drakṣyāmi nūnaṃ sukapolanāsikaṃ smitāvalokāruṇakañjalocanam mukhaṃ mukundasya guḍālakāvṛtaṃ pradakṣiṇaṃ me pracaranti vai mṛgāḥ

translation: Verily, as the deer go round by my right side (an auspicious omen), I believe I shall certainly behold today the face of Mukunda (Kṛṣṇa) with its charming cheeks and shapely nose, its smiling glances and reddish lotus-eyes, covered over with curly locks of hair.

devanagari: अप्यद्य विष्णोर्मनुजत्वमीयुषो । भारावताराय भुवो निजेच्छया । लावण्यधाम्नो भवितोपलम्भनं । मह्यं न न स्यात् फलमञ्जसा दृशः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: apyadya viṣṇormanujatvamīyuṣo bhārāvatārāya bhuvo nijecchayā lāvaṇyadhāmno bhavitopalambhanaṃ mahyaṃ na na syāt phalamañjasā dṛśaḥ

translation: How strongly I do yearn that today I shall be blessed with the audience (vision) of god Viṣṇu, the abode of beauty and excellence, who by his own Divine will has incarnated as a human being for lightening the burden of the earth. In case it so happens, it will be undeniable that the very existence of my eyes has achieved its fulfilment.

devanagari: य ईक्षिताहंरहितोऽप्यसत्सतोः । स्वतेजसापास्ततमोभिदाभ्रमः । स्वमाययाऽत्मन् रचितैस्तदीक्षया । प्राणाक्षधीभिः सदनेष्वभीयते ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: ya īkṣitāhaṃrahito svatejasāpāstatamobhidābhramaḥ svamāyayā prāṇākṣadhībhiḥ sadaneṣvabhīyate

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ rah class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ rah class 10 verb]

translation: Though he is merely the witness of the Cause and the effect, he is devoid of egoism. By virtue of his lustre (lustre-like Spiritual Knowledge), he has dispelled the darkness of ignorance or Nescience and the consequent notions of differentiation and misapprehensions resulting from it. By his will, he is, however, found to be sportively engaged in the houses (of Gopīs, arbours of trees in the Vṛndāvana forest) in the company of Jīvas created in himself through his Māyā Potency which, under his supervision and control, invests them with the life-spirit, the senses and mind or intellect.

devanagari: यस्याखिलामीवहभिः सुमङ्गलैः । वाचो विमिश्रा गुणकर्मजन्मभिः । प्राणन्ति शुम्भन्ति पुनन्ति वै जगत् । यास्तद्विरक्ताः शवशोभना मताः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: yasyākhilāmīvahabhiḥ sumaṅgalaiḥ vāco vimiśrā guṇakarmajanmabhiḥ prāṇanti śumbhanti punanti vai jagat yāstadviraktāḥ śavaśobhanā matāḥ

translation: The words describing his excellent attributes, exploits and incarnations which are capable of destroying all the sins of the world and of conferring auspicious blessings, resuscitate, contribute to the beauty and secure sanctity to the world. But words which do not describe them (howsoever ornamental and elegant they be) are like decorations of a dead body.

devanagari: स चावतीर्णः किल सत्वतान्वये । स्वसेतुपालामरवर्यशर्मकृत् । यशो वितन्वन् व्रज आस्त ईश्वरो । गायन्ति देवा यदशेषमङ्गलम् ॥ १३ ॥

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: The Almighty Lord who brings about the felicity and blessings to the great gods who abide by the injunctions laid down by him[2], has descended into the clan of the Sātvatas. It is reported that the Supreme Ruler now stays at Vraja spreading all around his glory, which, due to its being auspicious to all beings, is sung by gods.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā specifies these as varṇāśrama-dharma.

devanagari: तं त्वद्य नूनं महतां गतिं गुरुं । त्रैलोक्यकान्तं दृशिमन्महोत्सवम् । रूपं दधानं श्रिय ईप्सितास्पदं । द्रक्ष्ये ममासन्नुषसः सुदर्शनाः ॥...
english_devnagari: taṃ tvadya nūnaṃ mahatāṃ gatiṃ guruṃ trailokyakāntaṃ dṛśimanmahotsavam rūpaṃ dadhānaṃ śriya īpsitāspadaṃ drakṣye mamāsannuṣasaḥ sudarśanāḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ kam class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kam class 1 verb]

translation: Inasmuch as I have been witnessing auspicious omens from the dawn, I believe that to-day I shall certainly behold him—the one great goal of the noble souls, the Father (or the Preceptor) of the world, the most lovable and charming to the three worlds, a veritable grand feast to those who are blest with a sight—manifesting a form which is the coveted- most abode of the goddess Śrī.

devanagari: अथावरूढः सपदीशयो रथात् । प्रधानपुंसोश्चरणं स्वलब्धये । धिया धृतं योगिभिरप्यहं ध्रुवं । नमस्य आभ्यां च सखीन् वनौकसः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: athāvarūḍhaḥ sapadīśayo rathāt pradhānapuṃsoścaraṇaṃ svalabdhaye dhiyā dhṛtaṃ yogibhirapyahaṃ dhruvaṃ namasya ābhyāṃ ca sakhīn vanaukasaḥ
translation: And as soon as I see them, I shall immediately get down from the chariot and will certainly bow down directly to the feet of those two Almighty rulers of the Universe, the Prime Personalities—Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma whose feet have been merely intellectually grasped and meditated upon for Self-realization even by yogīs (but have never been able to visualize them actually). Along with them I shall pay respects to their cowherd-friends and to all the beings residing in the forest of Vṛndāvana.

devanagari: अप्यङ्घ्रिमूले पतितस्य मे विभुः । शिरस्यधास्यन् निजहस्तपङ्कजम् । दत्ताभयं कालभुजाङ्गरंहसा । प्रोद्वेजितानां शरणैषिणां णृनाम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: apyaṅghrimūle patitasya me vibhuḥ śirasyadhāsyan nijahastapaṅkajam dattābhayaṃ kālabhujāṅgaraṃhasā prodvejitānāṃ śaraṇaiṣiṇāṃ ṇṛnām
translation: (Now he covets the touch of the Lord’s hand and hopes that the Lord would fulfill his longing.) Will not that Omnipresent Lord place his lotus-palm on the head of mine who have prostrated myself at the soles of his feet—his hands which have afforded protection to all men who being terrified of the serpent in the form of Time (or Death) seek resort to them? (I heartily yearn for this favour).

devanagari: समर्हणं यत्र निधाय कौशिकः । तथा बलिश्चाप जगत्त्रयेन्द्रताम् । यद्वा विहारे व्रजयोषितां श्रमं । स्पर्शेन सौगन्धिकगन्ध्यपानुदत् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: samarhaṇaṃ yatra nidhāya kauśikaḥ tathā baliścāpa jagattrayendratām yadvā vihāre vrajayoṣitāṃ śramaṃ sparśena saugandhikagandhyapānudat

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ ud class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ ud class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ ud class 6 verb]

translation: By offering worship to those very palms Kauśika (Indra who is reported to have worshipped it on an occasion) and the demon-king Bali (who offered water in token of the gift of three paces of land to the Dwarf-Viṣṇu) attained sovereignty over the three worlds and which, by their very (cool and) fragrant touch like the Saugandhika lotus (noted for its specially sweet smell) alleviated the fatigue of the damsels of Vraja during the rāsa-sport and thus refreshed them.

devanagari: न मय्युपैष्यत्यरिबुद्धिमच्युतः । कंसस्य दूतः प्रहितोऽपि विश्वदृक् । योऽन्तर्बहिश्चेतस एतदीहितं । क्षेत्रज्ञ ईक्षत्यमलेन चक्षुषा ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: na mayyupaiṣyatyaribuddhimacyutaḥ kaṃsasya dūtaḥ prahito yo kṣetrajña īkṣatyamalena cakṣuṣā

translation: Though deputed by Kaṃsa, I am sure, he will entertain no hostile attitude to me as his enemy. For the Immortal[3] Lord is Omniscient and can see everything in the universe. Being the Supreme Soul (Kṣetrajña) he will see with his pure (unclouded and unprejudiced) vision what is desired inside and outside of the heart of everyone.

devanagari: अप्यङ्घ्रिमूलेऽवहितं कृताञ्जलिं । मामीक्षिता सस्मितमार्द्रया दृशा । सपद्यपध्वस्तसमस्तकिल्बिषो । वोढा मुदं वीतविशङ्क ऊर्जिताम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: apyaṅghrimūle māmīkṣitā sasmitamārdrayā dṛśā sapadyapadhvastasamastakilbiṣo voḍhā mudaṃ vītaviśaṅka ūrjitām

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [accusative single from √ ūrj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ ūrj class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ ūrj class 7 verb]

translation: How strongly I yearn for it! If he, with a merciful eye, looks smilingly at me who am prostrate at his feet, who with folded palms have concentrated on the soles of his feet, I shall instantaneously get thoroughly absolved of all sins, and with all my fears of saṃsāra dispelled, shall enjoy exquisite joy and Supreme bliss.

devanagari: सुहृत्तमं ज्ञातिमनन्यदैवतं । दोर्भ्यां बृहद्‍भ्यां परिरप्स्यतेऽथ माम् । आत्मा हि तीर्थीक्रियते तदैव मे । बन्धश्च कर्मात्मक उच्छ्वसित्य...
english_devnagari: suhṛttamaṃ jñātimananyadaivataṃ dorbhyāṃ bṛhad‍bhyāṃ parirapsyate ātmā hi tīrthīkriyate tadaiva me bandhaśca karmātmaka ucchvasityataḥ
translation: Would that when he would, with his mighty arras, embrace me, his best friend and well-wisher, his clansman who look upon him exclusively as my God, my soul (body) will be highly sanctified immediately and the fetters of karman lie shattered thenceforth.

devanagari: लब्ध्वाङ्गसङ्गं प्रणतं कृताञ्जलिं । मां वक्ष्यतेऽक्रूर ततेत्युरुश्रवाः । तदा वयं जन्मभृतो महीयसा । नैवादृतो यो धिगमुष्य जन्म तत् ॥ २१ ...

translation: When, after enjoying the privilege of being embraced by him (lit. having a personal contact with him), I shall stand bowing down to him with folded palms and he will address me, “Uncle Akrūra”, I shall feel that our coming into life (birth) has achieved its purpose. Fie upon the life of that being who is not respected in such a way by the worshipful Lord.

devanagari: न तस्य कश्चिद् दयितः सुहृत्तमो । न चाप्रियो द्वेष्य उपेक्ष्य एव वा । तथापि भक्तान् भजते यथा तथा । सुरद्रुमो यद्वदुपाश्रितोऽर्थदः ॥ २२ ...
english_devnagari: na tasya kaścid dayitaḥ suhṛttamo na cāpriyo dveṣya upekṣya eva vā tathāpi bhaktān bhajate yathā tathā suradrumo yadvadupāśrito
translation: No one is beloved of him or an intimate friend of him nor is any one disagreeable, hateful or worthy of indifferent treatment to him[4]. He however, attends upon his devotees, just as the celestial wish-yielding tree grants[5] the desire of a person when he is resorting to it.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Cf. samo'haṃ sarva-bhūteṣu na me dveṣyosti na priyaḥ etc.—Bhagavad Gītā 9.29.

Footnote [5]:
grants according to merits the deserving fruit—Padaratnāvalī (svasva-yogya-phaladānena)

devanagari: किं चाग्रजो मावनतं यदूत्तमः । स्मयन् परिष्वज्य गृहीतमञ्जलौ । गृहं प्रवेष्याप्तसमस्तसत्कृतं । सम्प्रक्ष्यते कंसकृतं स्वबन्धुषु ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ cāgrajo māvanataṃ yadūttamaḥ smayan pariṣvajya gṛhītamañjalau gṛhaṃ praveṣyāptasamastasatkṛtaṃ samprakṣyate kaṃsakṛtaṃ svabandhuṣu

translation: And moreover, his elder brother Balarāma. the best in the Yaḍu-clan, will embrace me with a smile. When I shall be humbly bowing with folded palms, he would take me by my hands and usher me into his house. When I shall be received with due formalities by all his relatives, he will enquire of Kaṃsa’s behaviour towards his relatives and clansmen.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इति सञ्चिन्तयन्कृष्णं श्वफल्कतनयोऽध्वनि । रथेन गोकुलं प्राप्तः सूर्यश्चास्तगिरिं नृप ॥ २४ ॥

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Oh King, while Akrūra, the son of Śvaphalka was contemplating about Lord Kṛṣṇa, he arrived by his chariot at Gokula, when the sun was reaching the setting mountain (the western horizon)

translation: In Vraja, he noticed the foot-prints of the Lord, the sacred dust of whose feet is respectfully received with their crowns (i.e. heads) by all the protectors of the worlds—footprints which are distinguished with the marks of a lotus, a barly seed, a goad and such other characteristics and are auspiciously ornamental to the earth.

devanagari: तद्दर्शनाह्लादविवृद्धसम्भ्रमः । प्रेम्णोर्ध्वरोमाश्रुकलाकुलेक्षणः । रथादवस्कन्द्य स तेष्वचेष्टत । प्रभोरमून्यङ्घ्रिरजांस्यहो इति ॥ २६ ...

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: His impatience heightened to its climax through the ecstasy of delight at their sight, his body thrilled with the excitement of love with his hair standing on ends, with his eyes bedimmed with tears (lit. tear-drops), he jumped from the chariot and exclaiming, “How blessed and rare are these particles of the dust from Lord’s feet”, he prostrated himself and rolled over them.

devanagari: देहंभृतामियानर्थो हित्वा दम्भं भियं शुचम् । सन्देशाद्यो हरेर्लिङ्ग दर्शनश्रवणादिभिः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: dehaṃbhṛtāmiyānartho hitvā dambhaṃ bhiyaṃ śucam sandeśādyo harerliṅga darśanaśravaṇādibhiḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]

translation: This is the real ultimate purpose of life (puruṣārtha) of all beings invested with a body that they emulate the ecstatic mental state and behaviour of Akrūra since the moment he received Kaṃsa’s order to bring Kṛṣṇa, without any hypocrisy, fear or worry at the sight of Hari’s image, hearing his glory etc.

devanagari: ददर्श कृष्णं रामं च व्रजे गोदोहनं गतौ । पीतनीलाम्बरधरौ शरदम्बुरहेक्षणौ ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: dadarśa kṛṣṇaṃ rāmaṃ ca vraje godohanaṃ gatau pītanīlāmbaradharau śaradamburahekṣaṇau

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: There, in the enclosure meant for milking cows, he saw Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma wearing yellow and blue garments respectively and with eyes beautiful like lotuses in the autumn.

devanagari: किशोरौ श्यामलश्वेतौ श्रीनिकेतौ बृहद्‍भुजौ । सुमुखौ सुन्दरवरौ बलद्विरदविक्रमौ ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: kiśorau śyāmalaśvetau śrīniketau bṛhad‍bhujau sumukhau sundaravarau baladviradavikramau
translation: They were Kiśora (lads between eleven to fifteen years) in age, dark-blue and white in complexion (respectively), the abodes of beauty and affluence (also bearing Śrī-vatsa mark in the case of Kṛṣṇa), with mighty arms and charming countenance, prominent among the beautiful, possessing the might and prowess of young elephants.

devanagari: ध्वजवज्राङ्कुशाम्भोजैः चिह्नितैरङ्घ्रिभिर्व्रजम् । शोभयन्तौ महात्मानौ सानुक्रोशस्मितेक्षणौ ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: dhvajavajrāṅkuśāmbhojaiḥ cihnitairaṅghribhirvrajam śobhayantau mahātmānau sānukrośasmitekṣaṇau

translation: The noble-souled pair of brothers ornamented the (ground in) Vraja with (the prints of) their feet marked with the (figures of) a banner, a Vajra, a goad and a lotus and their smiling eyes expressed compassion.

translation: They had taken their bath and had their person besmeared with fragrant pigments (such as sandal-paste). They wore wreaths of flower and Vanamālās and were immaculately attired. They expressed noble yet attractive playfulness.

devanagari: प्रधानपुरुषावाद्यौ जगद्धेतू जगत्पती । अवतीर्णौ जगत्यर्थे स्वांशेन बलकेशवौ ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: pradhānapuruṣāvādyau jagaddhetū jagatpatī avatīrṇau jagatyarthe svāṃśena balakeśavau

translation: They were the Foremost and the first ancient-most Persons, the cause of the world and the protectors thereof. They had descended on the world in their personal rays ( aṃśas) as Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa for the good of the world.

devanagari: दिशो वितिमिरा राजन् कुर्वाणौ प्रभया स्वया । यथा मारकतः शैलो रौप्यश्च कनकाचितौ ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: diśo vitimirā rājan kurvāṇau prabhayā svayā yathā mārakataḥ śailo raupyaśca kanakācitau

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: By their personal splendour they dispelled the darkness of the directions (all over the world). They appeared respectively like a mountain of emerald and a mount of silver both chased in gold[6], Oh King!
purport:
Footnote [6]:
The problem is how could the dark-blue Kṛṣṇa dispel darkness. Padaratnāvalī says Kṛṣṇa was like the sun and the ‘dark-splendour’ (Kṛṣṇa-prabhā) of the sun dispels darkness.
Bhāgavata Candrikā differs: Kṛṣṇa was like a big emerald set in the middle of gold while Balarāma was like a diamond inlaid in a dark background.

devanagari: रथात् तूर्णं अवप्लुत्य सोऽक्रूरः स्नेहविह्वलः । पपात चरणोपान्ते दण्डवत् रामकृष्णयोः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: rathāt tūrṇaṃ avaplutya so papāta caraṇopānte daṇḍavat rāmakṛṣṇayoḥ

translation: So overwhelmed with devout affection was that Akrūra that he quickly jumped down from the chariot, and fell prostrate like a stick near the feet of Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: भगवद्दर्शनाह्लाद बाष्पपर्याकुलेक्षणः । पुलकचिताङ्ग औत्कण्ठ्यात् स्वाख्याने नाशकन् नृप ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavaddarśanāhlāda bāṣpaparyākulekṣaṇaḥ pulakacitāṅga autkaṇṭhyāt svākhyāne nāśakan nṛpa
translation: With his eyes flooded with tears of joy at the sight of the glorious Lord and with hair standing all over his body, he was not able even to introduce himself to them as his throat was choked with love and emotion.

devanagari: भगवान् तमभिप्रेत्य रथाङ्गाङ्कितपाणिना । परिरेभेऽभ्युपाकृष्य प्रीतः प्रणतवत्सलः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavān tamabhipretya rathāṅgāṅkitapāṇinā parirebhe

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]

translation: Being affectionate to those who have completely surrendered themselves to him, the Lord who understood him (as to who he was and the purpose of his arrival etc.), drew Akrūra near him with his hands, characterised with the mark of discus, and embraced him as he was pleased with him.

devanagari: सङ्कर्षणश्च प्रणतं उपगुह्य महामनाः । गृहीत्वा पाणिना पाणी अनयत् सानुजो गृहम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: saṅkarṣaṇaśca praṇataṃ upaguhya mahāmanāḥ gṛhītvā pāṇinā pāṇī anayat sānujo gṛham
translation: The noble-minded Balarāma embraced Akrūra who was bowing low before him. Holding his clasped palms by his own, he led him to his house along with his younger brother Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: पृष्ट्वाथ स्वागतं तस्मै निवेद्य च वरासनम् । प्रक्षाल्य विधिवत् पादौ मधुपर्कार्हणमाहरत् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: pṛṣṭvātha svāgataṃ tasmai nivedya ca varāsanam prakṣālya vidhivat pādau madhuparkārhaṇamāharat
translation: After enquiring about his journey (whether he faced any troubles etc.) and offering him a high seat, Balarāma washed his feet with due formality and offered him articles of Madhuparka (such as honey, ghee, curds, water).

devanagari: निवेद्य गां चातिथये संवाह्य श्रान्तमादृतः । अन्नं बहुगुणं मेध्यं श्रद्धयोपाहरद् विभुः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: nivedya gāṃ cātithaye saṃvāhya śrāntamādṛtaḥ annaṃ bahuguṇaṃ medhyaṃ śraddhayopāharad vibhuḥ

translation: Having presented a cow to the guest and after massaging him as he was fatigued, the powerful Lord served to him with reverence, preparation of pure food with a variety of excellences.

devanagari: तस्मै भुक्तवते प्रीत्या रामः परमधर्मवित् । मखवासैर्गन्धमाल्यैः परां प्रीतिं व्यधात् पुनः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: tasmai bhuktavate prītyā rāmaḥ paramadharmavit makhavāsairgandhamālyaiḥ parāṃ prītiṃ vyadhāt punaḥ
translation: When his meal was over, Balarāma, the expert in the highest dharma (viz. proper reception of guest) again delighted him by offering articles like fragrant betel-leaf, scenting his mouth, perfumes and sweet-smelling garland.

devanagari: पप्रच्छ सत्कृतं नन्दः कथं स्थ निरनुग्रहे । कंसे जीवति दाशार्ह सौनपाला इवावयः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: papraccha satkṛtaṃ nandaḥ kathaṃ stha niranugrahe kaṃse jīvati dāśārha saunapālā ivāvayaḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: When the customary formalities were over, Nanda enquired: “Oh Dāśārha (Descendant of Daśārha)! How do you manage to get on with the regime of ruthless Kaṃsa—you who are now no better than sheep tended by a butcher.

devanagari: योऽवधीत् स्वस्वसुस्तोकान् क्रोशन्त्या असुतृप् खलः । किं नु स्वित्तत्प्रजानां वः कुशलं विमृशामहे ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: yo kiṃ nu svittatprajānāṃ vaḥ kuśalaṃ vimṛśāmahe

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [ablative single from √ kruś class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ kruś class 1 verb]

translation: That blood-thirsty wicked Kaṃsa has killed the children of his cousin (sister) even while she was bitterly crying. We wonder what safety or happiness there could be to you, his subjects.

devanagari: इत्थं सूनृतया वाचा नन्देन सुसभाजितः । अक्रूरः परिपृष्टेन जहावध्वपरिश्रमम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: itthaṃ sūnṛtayā vācā nandena susabhājitaḥ akrūraḥ paripṛṣṭena jahāvadhvapariśramam

[imperative active first dual], [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Having thus been honoured by Nanda in sweet words, Akrūra recovered from the fatigue of the journey by such polite enquiries.
